meta.title: Kitsune Cover — 限量聯名策展

dropsSection.lead: Each card works like an editorial product tile: clear drop status, partner-out links, and just enough context to feel collectible instead of disposable.

storiesSection.lead: The site wins not by saying “buy this,” but by explaining why a drop matters — design cues, franchise DNA, and collector appeal across markets.

shopGuide.lead: Kitsune Cover should present itself as a curated catalog for limited collab phone cases and accessories. Affiliate disclosure, brand distance, and editorial clarity should stay visible everywhere.

cards.title: Curated collab catalog
cards.body: Use language like showcase, selection, collector guide, and featured drop. Avoid posing as an official franchise store or a manufacturer.

cards.title: Partner-linked access
cards.body: Buttons should lead to partner merchants. Keep disclosure clean and plain. The value is curation, context, and visual selection — not pretending to hold inventory.

cards.title: Neo-pop editorial
cards.body: Dark premium base, hard black borders, loud accent stickers, oversized type, and modular blocks. That keeps it collectible instead of turning into a cheap anime shop.

story.jpVertical: 制作について · drop culture · tokyo street

footer.stack: Next.js · Tailwind · next-intl · 編輯式 DROP
footer.payLine: Line Pay · Apple Pay · Kakao Pay · Shop Pay

evangelion.sub: A limited EVANGELION case drop built for fans of bold design, collector energy, and iconic mecha visuals. Rare collab pieces, sharp detailing, and a release made to stand out.
evangelion.meta: ONLY 48 / 500 LEFT · JP / KR / TW / US
evangelion.heroImageAlt: EVANGELION × CASETiFY limited phone case — Rei Ayanami artwork
evangelion.label: Evangelion
evangelion.storyBody: Built on industrial caution-strip energy and sleek sci-fi tension, Evangelion collabs work when they feel engineered — not cutesy. The right case design should echo control panels, warning graphics, and emotional severity.

helloKitty.sub: The strongest Hello Kitty collabs are not childish — they are instantly legible pop artifacts. Pink, charm energy, glossy surfaces, and a collector mindset do the work here.
helloKitty.label: Hello Kitty
helloKitty.storyBody: Hello Kitty succeeds globally because it is more than kawaii. It is a visual language. Strong collabs know how to balance innocence, fashion gloss, and instantly collectible identity.

sailorMoon.sub: This drop direction leans into wand motifs, transformation compact silhouettes, and ornamental framing without drowning the page in glitter overload.
sailorMoon.label: Sailor Moon
sailorMoon.storyBody: The visual DNA of Sailor Moon is ceremonial and symbolic. The winning drops feel like compact jewelry translated into device form, not generic pastel merch.

ghost.sub: Minimal cyan, black systems language, and tactical interface fragments create a more adult collectible mood for users who want cyberpunk without cosplay energy.
ghost.label: Ghost in the Shell
ghost.storyTitle: Ghost in the Shell as collectible language
ghost.storyBody: This franchise rewards restraint. Hard geometry, interface typography, and signal-noise aesthetics feel truer than over-illustrated fan-service graphics.

doraemon.sub: The playful side of the site should still feel clean. Bright blue, circular gadget motifs, and iconic pocket cues are enough when the layout carries the premium weight.
doraemon.label: Doraemon
doraemon.storyBody: Doraemon collabs land when nostalgia stays crisp. Instead of cluttering the artwork, a few unmistakable symbols can carry the entire emotional load.

totoro.sub: Soft but not weak. This direction uses earthy green, roomy compositions, and quiet iconography to make the page feel intimate and collectible rather than childish.
totoro.label: Totoro
totoro.storyBody: Totoro works best when the design leaves room for silence. You do not need visual noise when the character memory is already strong enough.

tbd.sub: Reserve one slot in the system for future launches. That makes the site feel ongoing, collectible, and editorially alive instead of frozen after first publish.
tbd.meta: Launch queue open · add to watchlist
tbd.label: Upcoming
tbd.storyBody: A reserved future slot helps the homepage read like a living magazine. It suggests cadence, curation, and an expectation of the next cultural object.
